name: aphrodite (sometimes)
shortDescription: Aphrodite is the ancient Greek goddess of love, beauty, pleasure, and procreation. She is often associated with desire and sexuality.
description: Aphrodite is the ancient Greek goddess of love, beauty, pleasure, and procreation. There are two main traditions regarding her birth: one, found in Hesiod's Theogony, claims she was born from the sea foam created when Cronus castrated Uranus and threw his genitals into the sea; the other, found in Homer's Iliad, presents her as the daughter of Zeus and Dione. Regardless of her parentage, Aphrodite embodies the irresistible power of desire and beauty. She is often depicted as a stunningly beautiful woman, sometimes attended by the Erotes (winged gods of love). Her influence extends to all aspects of life involving attraction and affection, from romantic love to sexual desire. Aphrodite is married to Hephaestus, the god of the forge, but is famously unfaithful, engaging in affairs with gods like Ares and mortals like Anchises (with whom she conceived Aeneas). Her role in the Trojan War, siding with the Trojans, further illustrates her involvement in human affairs and the consequences of love and beauty. While her power is undeniable, Aphrodite is sometimes depicted as vain and easily swayed by emotion. Her worship was widespread in ancient Greece, with temples dedicated to her in cities like Corinth and Paphos.
